burn_rate_method: SAMA foreign assets treated as primary liquid buffer; PIF ($700bn) excluded as long-term equity fund not readily deployable for deficit financing; burn estimated from IMF Article IV fiscal deficit at below-breakeven scenario annualised and divided by 12
notes: Liquid buffer = SAMA assets (~$425bn); PIF AUM ~$700bn but illiquid equity; burn reflects partial fiscal adjustment

burn_rate_method: CBI FX reserves only; Iraq has no significant SWF; burn estimated from IMF Article IV fiscal deficit at below-breakeven oil price scenario; Iraq's spending is largely non-discretionary (wages/pensions ~75% of budget)
source_fx: IMF WEO 2023; Central Bank of Iraq
notes: No SWF; FX reserves highly sensitive to oil price; high non-discretionary spending limits burn-rate flexibility

burn_rate_method: KGF (General Reserve Fund) accessible for deficit financing; RFFG (~85% of KIA AUM) constitutionally protected — excluded from liquid buffer; burn from IMF Article IV estimates at below-breakeven scenario
source_swf: KIA Annual Report (estimated); Kuwait Ministry of Finance
notes: RFFG (~$648bn of KIA) legally ring-fenced; accessible GRF ~$60bn; very low burn relative to buffer

burn_rate_method: LIA assets partially frozen or illiquid; CBL data fragmented by dual-authority structure; liquid buffer conservative estimate; burn from IMF REO lower bound; revenues and spending split between eastern and western governments
source_fx: IMF REO MENA 2023 (estimate; dual-CBL complicates data)
notes: Highly uncertain; political fragmentation; asset freeze; dual-government spending makes burn estimate very approximate

burn_rate_method: FRR (Fund for Revenue Regulation) essentially depleted by 2017; Banque d'Algerie FX reserves are primary buffer; burn from IMF Article IV at below-breakeven scenario reflecting large subsidy and wage bill
source_fx: IMF WEO 2023; Banque d'Algerie Annual Report 2023
notes: FRR nearly exhausted; FX reserves declining trend; 25-month runway is a warning signal at current burn rate

burn_rate_method: CBUAE FX reserves plus accessible ADAA government deposits; combined SWF (ADIA ~$790bn + Mubadala ~$280bn + ADQ ~$110bn) largely long-term illiquid equity; burn from IMF Article IV UAE estimates; Abu Dhabi production base limits fiscal stress
source_swf: ADIA 2023; Mubadala Annual Report 2023; ADQ 2023 (all estimated)
notes: Total AUM very large but mostly illiquid; accessible buffer much smaller; diversified economy limits fiscal oil dependency

burn_rate_method: QCB reserves plus estimated accessible QIA allocation for budget support; majority of QIA in long-term global equity; burn from IMF Article IV at below-breakeven LNG-linked scenario
notes: QIA AUM ~$475bn but illiquid; accessible portion small; LNG-linked revenues provide some buffer vs Brent correlation

burn_rate_method: OIA (SGRF ~$16bn + OIF ~$4bn) plus CBO FX reserves treated as combined accessible buffer; burn from IMF Article IV at below-breakeven scenario; fiscal consolidation since 2020 has reduced structural burn rate
notes: Post-2020 fiscal reforms reduced structural deficit; 30-month runway adequate for medium-term adjustment

burn_rate_method: NDFI (National Development Fund) severely constrained by US sanctions; CBI FX reserves partially frozen or inaccessible; liquid buffer represents lower bound of accessible assets; burn highly uncertain
source_fx: IMF REO MENA 2023 (estimate; very low confidence — CBI data withheld under sanctions)
notes: Extremely uncertain; frozen assets not included; parallel exchange rate distorts USD conversion; sanctions regime makes burn estimate unreliable

burn_rate_method: CBB FX reserves as primary liquid buffer; Mumtalakat holdings (~$18bn) are long-term equity investments excluded from runway; GCC Development Fund grants/loans not counted as buffer; burn from IMF Article IV at below-breakeven scenario
notes: Highest per-barrel breakeven in GCC; thin CBB buffer; GCC Development Fund support is off-balance-sheet and not guaranteed

burn_rate_method: CBE FX reserves as primary accessible buffer; TSFE (~$11bn) and sovereign-adjacent holdings excluded as illiquid; burn based on LNG export revenue sensitivity and subsidy cost dynamics at below-breakeven scenario; mixed_importer dynamics make burn less predictable
notes: Mixed exporter/importer dynamics; LNG exports provide partial offset; run-rate sensitive to IMF programme conditionality

burn_rate_method: N/A — net importer; FX reserves shown for macroeconomic reference only; fiscal breakeven concept not applicable; Jordan faces import cost shock not oil revenue shortfall
notes: Net oil importer; reserve runway concept not applicable; FX reserves cover ~8 months of imports

burn_rate_method: N/A — net importer; BDL FX data highly uncertain given ongoing economic and financial crisis; figures represent best available estimate; reserve runway concept not applicable
source_fx: IMF REO MENA 2023 (estimate; very low confidence — BDL data withheld)
notes: Net importer in severe economic crisis; BDL data unreliable; much of stated reserves may be pledged or encumbered

burn_rate_method: N/A — net importer; BAM FX reserves shown for reference; fiscal stress driven by energy import costs not oil revenue; reserve runway concept not applicable
